site.title: Course Peer Observation
site.subtitle: Fundamentals of Computer Science
site.description: A collaborative peer observation program for the Fundamentals of Computer Science course, fostering teaching excellence through constructive feedback and shared learning.

home.hero_description: Enhancing teaching quality through collaborative observation, reflection, and professional development in computer science education.

items.title: Collaborative Learning
items.description: Peers observe and learn from each other's teaching methods, promoting a culture of continuous improvement.

items.title: Constructive Feedback
items.description: Structured observation forms and post-observation discussions help identify strengths and areas for growth.

items.title: Professional Development
items.description: Participants gain insights into diverse teaching strategies and pedagogical approaches in CS education.

items.title: EMI Enhancement
items.description: Support English as a Medium of Instruction (EMI) teaching practices with peer-driven quality assurance.

emi_community.title: EMI Community Plan
emi_community.subtitle: NCHU EMI Teaching Resource Center — Cross-University Bilingual Professional Development Community
emi_community.intro: This community is a Type C (Course Quality Assurance) group, composed of EMI instructors from two or more universities, dedicated to enhancing EMI teaching quality through course quality assurance mechanisms.

members.name: Hsueh-Ting Chu
members.role: Associate Prof. & Director, Intl. Informatics Program

members.department: College of Informatics, Dept. of AI Applications

members.name: Ching-Wen Huang

activities: 1 external expert (Prof. Yi Zha, Chung Yuan Christian University)
qa_items: Administer NCHU EMI Course Teaching Feedback Questionnaire with statistical and quantitative analysis
Classroom observation: observers complete the Course QA Community Observation Form
Data correlation analysis of midterm/final exam results (score distribution, Chinese/English teaching effectiveness comparison, etc.)
emi_community.rubric_description: Revised based on the EMI Course Quality Assurance Standard Rubric provided by the NCHU EMI Teaching Resource Center, following the center's rubric review process.

about.title: Peer Observation Plan
about.intro: The Course Peer Observation program for Fundamentals of Computer Science is designed to enhance teaching quality through a structured, supportive peer review process. This initiative brings together instructors to observe, reflect, and improve their teaching practices in an EMI (English as a Medium of Instruction) context.
objectives: Promote reflective teaching practices among CS educators
Provide constructive, evidence-based feedback on teaching
Share effective teaching strategies for CS foundation courses
Enhance EMI teaching skills through peer collaboration
Build a supportive community of practice in CS education

process_steps.title: Pre-Observation Meeting
process_steps.description: Observer and instructor meet to discuss the lesson plan, learning objectives, and specific areas for feedback.

process_steps.title: Classroom Observation
process_steps.description: The observer attends the class session, taking notes on teaching methods, student engagement, and learning activities.

process_steps.title: Post-Observation Discussion
process_steps.description: A reflective discussion where both parties share observations, celebrate strengths, and identify opportunities for improvement.

process_steps.title: Written Feedback
process_steps.description: The observer provides a structured written report summarizing key observations and recommendations.

principles: Confidentiality — Observations are for professional development, not evaluation
Respect — All feedback is delivered constructively and collegially
Reciprocity — Every participant serves as both observer and observed
Growth — The focus is on continuous improvement, not perfection
